data.label: Move (trajectory action, streaming feedback)
data.description: ROS `send_action_goal` to `/xarm6_traj_controller/follow_joint_trajectory` (`control_msgs/action/FollowJointTrajectory`). Commands a single-waypoint trajectory to the proven-reachable joint target [0, -0.3, -0.3, 0, 0.6, 0] (reused from demo 30) on the xArm 6 driven through ros2_control fake hardware. This is a STREAMING action node (`streamOutput: true`): as the controller follows the trajectory it publishes structured `FollowJointTrajectory_Feedback` frames (`{ header, joint_names, desired, actual, error }` — each a nested JointTrajectoryPoint), far richer than the turtle's scalar `{ remaining }`. The backend dedups the DUPLICATED frames rosbridge emits and emits each DISTINCT feedback as a streamed `OutputSet` on the node's `stream` handle; the slim control token carries `stream_count == number of distinct feedbacks` over the `control` edge to size the fold's end-of-stream gather barrier. The terminal action RESULT (`error_code`) rides `stdout_tail` ONLY — it is NOT a referenceable output, and success is implicit (the backend only succeeds when GoalStatus == 4 SUCCEEDED). The goal is authored as free-form JSON in `config.fields` and passed to rosbridge verbatim (nested array-of-structs, no mapper involvement); `interface_typedefs` carries the flat `FollowJointTrajectory_Result` typedef so the deriver can shape the result port for this non-bundled action. Runs on an `xarm_fleet` runner that satisfies `ros.robot_model == xarm6`; the rosbridge endpoint is runner-local (`EXECUTOR_ROS__WS_URL`).

data.label: Fold Feedback
data.description: Drains the trajectory action's feedback stream (one structured `FollowJointTrajectory_Feedback` chunk per distinct feedback) and reduces it into an ordered Array, gating net completion on the end-of-stream count carried on the move node's control token.
